Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a scenario We have a temporal profile model dictionary mapping pairs of activities to average time in seconds and standard deviation We need to identify anomalies generate hypotheses and propose SQL verification queries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt says Do not reference the instructions or explanations above Present the anomalies possible reasons and verification queries independently

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus we need to output A list or description of anomalies observed from the temporal profile Hypotheses explaining why each anomaly might exist possible reasons Proposed verification approaches SQL queries to test those hypotheses

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets analyze the temporal profile R to P avg about 25 hours stdev 1 hour low stdev P to N avg 7 days stdev 2 days very long and variable A to C avg 2 hours stdev 1 hour short E to N avg 5 min stdev 1 min very fast E to C avg 1 hour stdev 50 min high relative variability

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can highlight R to P average time about 25 hours but standard deviation only 1 hour That is unusually low variability suggests a rigid automated process or artificially enforced timing

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: P to N average 7 days standard deviation 2 days Very long delay high variability Could be bottleneck in notification process or notifications are batched weekly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A to C average 2 hours standard deviation 1 hour That is a short time from assign to close but the expected process should go through evaluate approve notify then close So perhaps some claims are closed directly after assignment without evaluation approval or they are expedited

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: E to N average 5 minutes standard deviation 1 minute Very fast notification after evaluation maybe automated or skipping intermediate approval step

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Low stdev in R to P maybe an automated approval process that runs daily at a fixed time or a policy that requires exactly about 25 hours before approval Alternatively it could be due to manual data entry where the approval timestamp is artificially set to match a scheduled report

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: High delay in P to N notifications may be handled by a separate team with limited capacity leading to a backlog Or notifications are only sent after a mandatory waiting period The high variability could be due to different adjuster workloads or claim complexities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Quick A to C claims might be automatically closed if they are below a certain monetary threshold or if they are deemed invalid during assignment Also some adjusters might be closing claims prematurely to meet quotas

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Fast E to N possibly an automated system sends a notification as soon as the evaluation is recorded This suggests that notification does not wait for approval which contradicts the expected process if approval is required Could indicate two process variants one where evaluation leads directly to notification and another where approval is needed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Large variability for E to C after evaluation some claims are closed quickly while others go through additional steps before closure leading to longer times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to use the database schema claims adjusters claim_events The queries should refer to those tables

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For R to P anomaly compute the time difference between Receive and Approve for each claim compare to the average 90000 seconds and stdev 3600 and select claims outside a threshold such as 2 standard deviations Then join with claims to see if certain claim types or amounts correlate

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For P to N delay get time between Approve and Notify flag long delays and join with adjusters or resource information to see if certain adjusters cause longer delays

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For A to C short duration check claims where time between Assign and Close is less than a threshold and see if there are missing intermediate events Evaluate Approve Notify This would indicate skipping

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For E to N fast duration get time between Evaluate and Notify flag very short durations and check if those claims also have an Approve step before Notify

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For E to C high variability compute duration between Evaluate and Close and group by claim type or amount to separate fast and slow populations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: These queries cover identifying claims where time between activities falls outside expected ranges correlating anomalies with claim types adjusters resources and filtering claims closed immediately after assignment or where approval to notification takes excessively long
